qa_pairs.question: When was the Cedar compute cluster retired?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar compute cluster was retired on September 12, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Do users need to migrate files from Cedar to Fir?
qa_pairs.answer: No, files stored on Cedar are already available on Fir because the two clusters share the same file systems, so no action is required regarding your stored files.

qa_pairs.question: After Cedar's retirement, where should users submit new jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: Starting September 12, users should submit their jobs to another cluster on the new national infrastructure, including Fir.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Graham compute cluster retired?
qa_pairs.answer: The Graham compute cluster was retired on September 1, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Is it necessary for users to migrate files from Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: No, files stored on Graham are already available on Nibi because the two clusters share the same file systems, so no action is required.

qa_pairs.question: Where should users submit jobs after Graham's retirement?
qa_pairs.answer: Starting September 1, users should submit their jobs to another cluster on the new national infrastructure, including Nibi.

qa_pairs.question: Will the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application process change due to the infrastructure transition?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application process remains the same, although the competition itself will be impacted by the transition.

qa_pairs.question: What will happen to 2024/25 RAC allocations during the infrastructure transition?
qa_pairs.answer: 2024/25 RAC allocations will remain in effect on retiring clusters as long as each cluster remains in service.

qa_pairs.question: When will the 2025/26 RAC allocations be implemented?
qa_pairs.answer: The 2025/26 RAC allocations will be implemented everywhere once all new clusters are in service.

qa_pairs.question: What can users with both 2024 and 2025 RAC awards expect during the transition period?
qa_pairs.answer: Users holding both a 2024 and a 2025 RAC award may experience a period when neither award is available because old clusters will mostly be out of service before all new ones are available.

qa_pairs.question: Can users compute on new clusters before their 2025 RAC allocations are fully available?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, users will be able to compute with their default allocation (def-xxxxxx) on each new cluster as soon as it goes into service, but the 2025 RAC allocations will only become available when all new clusters are in service.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more training opportunities related to the Advanced Research Computing infrastructure?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can discover more training opportunities through Explora, accessible at https://explora.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the 'Introduction to HPC on Alliance Clusters' course about?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Introduction to HPC on Alliance Clusters' is a 3-part webinar series covering hardware, tools, software environment, compiling codes, using Slurm, benchmarking, and managing permissions for new or prospective HPC users on Canada's DRI systems.

qa_pairs.question: Who instructs the 'Introduction to HPC on Alliance Clusters' webinar series?
qa_pairs.answer: Alex Razoumov from Simon Fraser University (SFU) / West DRI instructs the 'Introduction to HPC on Alliance Clusters' webinar series.

qa_pairs.question: When were the 'Introduction to HPC on Alliance Clusters' webinars scheduled?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Introduction to HPC on Alliance Clusters' webinars were scheduled for Thursdays: September 25, October 2, and October 9, 2025, at 10:00 AM PT.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the 'HPC105: Intro to SciNet and Trillium' course?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'HPC105: Intro to SciNet and Trillium' is a self-guided online course designed to help prospective users of Trillium and new users to SciNet with account setup, first login, running computations, and workflow migration from legacy systems like Niagara and Mist.

qa_pairs.question: Who provides the 'HPC105: Intro to SciNet and Trillium' course?
qa_pairs.answer: SciNet provides the 'HPC105: Intro to SciNet and Trillium' course, with instruction from the SciNet Education Team.

qa_pairs.question: When was the 'Migrating to the upgraded national systems' webinar held?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Migrating to the upgraded national systems' webinar was held on Wednesday, July 30, 2025, at 12:00 PM EDT.

qa_pairs.question: Which old clusters were replaced by new ones, as discussed in the 'Migrating to the upgraded national systems' webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: According to the webinar, Graham became Nibi, Beluga became Rorqual, Cedar became Fir, and Niagara became Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: What was the 'Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC' webinar about?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC' webinar highlighted tools such as in-situ visualization, lossy 3D data compression, distributed storage, and DAR (Disk ARchiver) to enhance research workflows involving large datasets.

qa_pairs.question: Who presented the 'Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC' webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Alex Razoumov from Simon Fraser University (SFU) / West DRI presented the 'Workflow Hacks for Large Datasets in HPC' webinar.

qa_pairs.question: What is the 'Mastering GPU Efficiency' course designed to teach?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Mastering GPU Efficiency' course provides basic training on using GPUs on national systems, focusing on measuring GPU utilization and employing NVIDIA technologies like MPS (Multi-Process Service) and MIG (Multi-Instance GPU) to improve efficiency.

qa_pairs.question: Who is the 'Mastering GPU Efficiency' course intended for?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Mastering GPU Efficiency' course is intended for prospective users of the upgraded systems, specifically Alliance users working with GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: What topics are covered in the 'Introduction to the Fir cluster' webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Introduction to the Fir cluster' webinar provides an overview of the cluster, its hardware, filesystems and their recommended usage, job submission policies, and overall best practices for using the cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Who presented the 'Introduction to the Fir cluster' webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: Alex Razoumov from Simon Fraser University (SFU) / West DRI presented the 'Introduction to the Fir cluster' webinar.

qa_pairs.question: When was the 'Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades' webinar held?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades' webinar was held on Wednesday, November 20, 2024, from 12:00 PM to 1:00 PM ET.

qa_pairs.question: What challenge did the 'Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades' webinar address regarding the new H100 GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: The webinar addressed the significant challenge that while total GPU computing power would grow, the number of GPUs would decrease from 3200 to 2100, requiring new strategies for efficient utilization.

qa_pairs.question: What NVIDIA technologies were discussed in the 'Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades' to improve GPU utilization?
qa_pairs.answer: The webinar discussed NVIDIA's MPS (Multi-Process Service) and MIG (Multi-Instance GPU) technologies as ways to improve GPU utilization and mitigate the challenge of fewer GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: Who was the 'Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades' webinar for?
qa_pairs.answer: The webinar was for prospective users of the upgraded systems, especially those intending to use a substantial amount of H100 resources (e.g., more than one GPU at a time, and/or over 24 hours runtime).
